---
name: scaffold-stories-from-epic
description: >
  Parses an epic file's section 7 (Candidate stories) to extract story outlines and generates
  detailed story MD files with all required sections. Updates the epic file with markdown links
  to the generated stories and syncs the backlog index.
  Use this skill when an epic has section 7 populated and you need to generate story files from it.
---

# Skill: scaffold-stories-from-epic

## Purpose

Generate `story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md` files from an epic's **Section 7 — Candidate stories**
outlines and write them to `docs/agile/stories/`. After generation, update the epic file to convert
plain-text story headings into markdown links pointing to the new files.

---

## Inputs

| Input | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `EPIC_FILE` | Absolute path to the epic markdown file | `/.../.../docs/epics/Epic 1 — Baseline Implementation.md` |
| `AGILE_DIR` | Absolute path to `docs/agile/` | `/.../.../docs/agile` |
| `EPIC_NUMBER` | Epic number (integer) used in story IDs | `1` |

---

## Process

### Phase 1 — Read and parse the epic file

1. Read `EPIC_FILE` completely.
2. Locate **Section 7 — Candidate stories** (may be titled "7. Candidate stories", "## 7)", or similar).
3. Extract each story outline block. A block consists of:
   - A `###` heading with the story title (e.g., `### Story 1 — Server-side Filtering`)
   - An optional description paragraph immediately after the heading
   - An optional key outputs / notes list
4. Assign sequential story numbers: the first outline → `S1`, the second → `S2`, etc.
   - Cross-check with existing story files in `AGILE_DIR/stories/` to avoid ID collisions.
   - If `story-E<epic>S<n>-*.md` already exists for a given `n`, skip and log a warning.

### Phase 2 — Generate slug and file path

For each story outline:

1. Derive the **slug** from the story title:
   - Lowercase, strip punctuation, replace spaces with hyphens.
   - Limit to 40 characters. Truncate at a word boundary.
   - Example: `"Server-side Filtering for Feature Flags"` → `server-side-filtering-for-feature-flags`
2. Compose the file name: `story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md`
   - Example: `story-E1S1-server-side-filtering-for-feature-flags.md`
3. Compose the output path: `AGILE_DIR/stories/<file-name>`

### Phase 3 — Fill and write each story file

Use the **Story Template** (see below) to produce each file. Substitution rules:

| Placeholder | Value |
|---|---|
| `{{STORY_ID}}` | `E<epic>-S<n>` (e.g., `E1-S1`) |
| `{{EPIC_TITLE}}` | Epic title as found in the epic file's `# ` heading |
| `{{EPIC_FILE_PATH}}` | Relative path from the story file to the epic MD |
| `{{STORY_TITLE}}` | Story title from the outline heading |
| `{{STORY_SLUG}}` | Derived slug |
| `{{RESPONSIBLE_AGENT}}` | `TBD` (leave for human/agent review after creation) |
| `{{SKILLS}}` | `TBD` |
| `{{INSTRUCTIONS}}` | `TBD` |
| `{{DEPENDS_ON}}` | `—` (or adjust if epic notes a dependency) |
| `{{BLOCKS}}` | `—` |
| `{{CREATED_AT}}` | Current timestamp in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS -HH` format (use `node docs/.github/functions/datetime.js`) |
| `{{USER_STORY_DESCRIPTION}}` | Derived from the outline description; use `TBD` if not available |
| `{{SCOPE_ITEMS}}` | Bullet list derived from key outputs in the outline; use `TBD` if not available |

Write the file. Log the path to stdout:
```
✅ Created: docs/agile/stories/story-E1S1-server-side-filtering-for-feature-flags.md
```

### Phase 4 — Update the epic file

For each generated story:

1. Find the corresponding `###` heading in section 7 of the epic file.
2. If it is **plain text**, replace it with a markdown link:
   ```
   ### [Story N — Title](../agile/stories/story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md)
   ```
   > Path is relative from the epic's location (`docs/epics/`) to `docs/agile/stories/`.
3. If the heading is **already a markdown link**, skip (idempotent).
4. Write the updated epic file back.
5. Log: `🔗 Updated epic link: Story N → story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md`

### Phase 5 — Sync backlog index

Run the backlog sync to register all new story IDs:

```bash
node "docs/.github/functions/sync-backlog-index.js" "docs/agile"
```

If the script exits non-zero, log the error and stop. **Do not proceed with committing** until the sync passes.

---

## Outputs

| Artifact | Location | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Story MD files | `docs/agile/stories/story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md` | One per outline parsed |
| Updated epic file | `docs/epics/<epic-file>.md` | Section 7 headings converted to links |
| Updated backlog index | `docs/agile/backlog-index.json` | Regenerated by `sync-backlog-index.js` |

---

## Story Template

Use the following template verbatim. Replace all `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` tokens before writing.

````markdown
# Story {{STORY_ID}} — {{STORY_TITLE}}

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **ID** | {{STORY_ID}} |
| **Epic** | [{{EPIC_TITLE}}]({{EPIC_FILE_PATH}}) |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Status** | Draft |
| **Responsible agent** | `{{RESPONSIBLE_AGENT}}` |
| **Skills** | `{{SKILLS}}` |
| **Instructions** | `{{INSTRUCTIONS}}` |
| **Depends on** | {{DEPENDS_ON}} |
| **Blocks** | {{BLOCKS}} |
| Created at | {{CREATED_AT}} |
| Last updated | {{CREATED_AT}} |

---

## 1) User story

**As a** {{USER_STORY_DESCRIPTION}}

---

## 2) Scope

### In scope

{{SCOPE_ITEMS}}

### Out of scope

1. TBD — populate before story refinement.

---

## 3) Acceptance criteria

### AC-1 — TBD

- **Given** TBD
- **When** TBD
- **Then** TBD

---

## 4) Tasks

> Tasks to be defined during story refinement. Use `create-story-task-pack` skill when section 4 is populated.

```
*(replace this block and the closing triple-backtick with actual task entries when ready)*
```
---
````

> **Note:** Remove the inner code block wrapper when writing the actual story file. The template is shown
> in a code block here only for display purposes.

---

## Constraints

1. **Never generate a story without at minimum:** `ID`, `Priority`, `Status`, and `Responsible agent` (value `TBD` is acceptable for `Responsible agent` at generation time).
2. **Always set `Status: Draft`** for newly generated stories. Never set `In Progress` or `Done` at generation time.
3. **Always include `## 4) Tasks`** section header, even if the body is empty or contains only the placeholder comment.
4. **Never overwrite an existing story file.** If the target path already exists, log a warning and skip.
5. **Never create circular dependencies.** If the outline notes a dependency that would create a cycle, set the field to `TBD` and log a warning.
6. **Always run `sync-backlog-index.js` after writing all files** (Phase 5). Do not commit without a clean sync exit.
7. **All text must be in English** (titles, descriptions, acceptance criteria, scope items).

---

## Validation checklist

After completing all phases, verify:

- [ ] Every story file exists at `docs/agile/stories/story-E<epic>S<n>-<slug>.md`
- [ ] Every story has all required metadata fields (no blanks — `TBD` is acceptable for agents/skills)
- [ ] Every story has `Status: Draft`
- [ ] Every story has a `## 4) Tasks` section
- [ ] Epic section 7 has markdown links for every generated story
- [ ] `sync-backlog-index.js` exits 0

---

## Anti-patterns to avoid

- **Never skip Phase 5.** Backlog index must be synced before any commit.
- **Never generate stories from sections other than section 7.** Only canonical candidate story outlines belong here.
- **Never set `Priority: P0`** for generated stories without explicit justification in the outline.
- **Never use hardcoded timestamps.** Always call `datetime.js` to get the current time.
- **Never produce a story with fewer than 3 sections.** Minimum: metadata + user story + `## 4) Tasks` placeholder.
